old_claim.note: Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency is retained between Lateen Sail & Caravel and Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation; Caravel design does not require portolan charts as a direct prerequisite; charts are navigational aids, not ship-construction prerequisites.

source_shape.source_locator: Article describes the caravel as a light sailing ship of the 15th to 17th centuries, developed by the Portuguese for African coastal exploration and able to sail to windward.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Caravel design does not require portolan charts as a direct prerequisite; charts are navigational aids, not ship-construction prerequisites.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The source supports the scoped Lateen Sail & Caravel claim or adjacent chronology without requiring Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation as a prerequisite.

ontology_before: Lateen Sail & Caravel treated Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation as a direct prerequisite.
ontology_after: Lateen Sail & Caravel no longer directly depends on Portolan Charts & Maritime Navigation; the dependency boundary is narrowed to operationally necessary predecessors.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant changed: lateen_sail_caravel must not directly depend on portolan_charts_maritime_navigation unless a narrower source-backed node scope is introduced.
would_reject_if: A source shows lateen_sail_caravel is intentionally scoped only to cases that require portolan_charts_maritime_navigation.
The graph splits lateen_sail_caravel and reserves this id for a narrower technology that directly depends on portolan_charts_maritime_navigation.
A stronger source demonstrates that portolan_charts_maritime_navigation is chronologically and causally necessary for lateen_sail_caravel.
short_reason: Caravel design does not require portolan charts as a direct prerequisite; charts are navigational aids, not ship-construction prerequisites.
